steps.title: Factor out 3 from the left side
steps.content:
The expression $3x - 18$ can be written as $3(x - 6)$. The equation becomes:

$$3(x - 6) = -12$$

Since we're asked to find $x - 6$, this factorization reveals exactly what we need.

steps.title: Divide both sides by 3 to isolate x - 6
steps.content:
Dividing both sides by 3:

$$\frac{3(x - 6)}{3} = \frac{-12}{3}$$

$$x - 6 = -4$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
